description: Explicit classifications for port-substance audit rows that are THIN, MISSING, WRAPPER, or HOLLOW. The gate requires exact module coverage and at least one known conformance test id per row.

entries.reason: Python wrapper facade is represented by the native DCEC prover and policy-to-DCEC TS path rather than a same-name wrapper.
English DCEC wrapper behavior is consolidated into the TS DCEC parser/prover path and native DCEC vectors.
Integration facade is intentionally smaller in TS and delegates to local TS DCEC services, with DCEC native vectors covering the decision path.
The TS grammar parser keeps the deterministic parsing surface compact while DCEC and legal norm vectors exercise the consuming behavior.
Base inference rule abstractions are folded into TS DCEC prover data structures and rule evaluation helpers.
Propositional DCEC rule behavior is consolidated into TS DCEC prover execution instead of a same-name rule module.
Problem parsing is compact in TS and exercised through parser command and DCEC native conformance vectors.
Proof optimization is folded into TS proof/result normalization rather than a separate module boundary.
Extended DCEC rules are represented inside the TS core command/prover path and checked by DCEC native vectors.
NL policy compilation is split across TS grammar and policy compiler services; DCEC/legal norm vectors cover the generated policy behavior.
E prover integration is an external host-native binary adapter, not a self-contained WASM/TS prover target.
Vampire integration is an external host-native binary adapter, not a self-contained WASM/TS prover target.
Talos wrapper depends on an external prover runtime and is tracked as host-native rather than simulated TS parity.
TDFOL rule base abstractions are folded into the TS TDFOL bridge and policy-to-TDFOL serializer.
First-order TDFOL rule behavior is consolidated into the TS TDFOL bridge execution path.
Propositional TDFOL rules are represented in the TS TDFOL prover bridge rather than a same-name module.
LLM-assisted NL behavior is non-deterministic integration glue and excluded from deterministic prover parity; deterministic TDFOL decisions are covered separately.
Profiling/reporting volume is not theorem-prover semantics; the TS profiler is compact while TDFOL decisions are covered by native vectors.
Strategy base classes are folded into TS prover strategy selection and bridge options.
CEC delegation strategy is represented by TS DCEC/TDFOL bridge routing rather than a Python-shaped strategy module.
Strategy selection is consolidated into TS bridge options and deterministic native TDFOL dispatch.
TDFOL conversion is implemented through TS policy-to-TDFOL and SMT2 serialization modules.
TDFOL inference rules are consolidated into the TS TDFOL bridge decision engine.
Proof cache behavior is represented by TS bridge-level result normalization and does not need a same-name port.
Python API facade is represented by SwissKnife prover hub entrypoints and conformance runners rather than a same-name TS file.
Server wrapper is deployment glue outside prover semantics; prover behavior is tested through the TS hub and native vectors.
Benchmark harness code is measurement scaffolding, not theorem-prover behavior; native vectors cover decisions.
CEC-DCEC bridge behavior is compacted into TS namespace/bridge services and exercised by DCEC native conformance vectors.
Deontic-norm bridge helpers are compact TS implementations with dedicated cross-language parity slices for extraction, normalization, guidance, and row matching.
FOL-to-TDFOL bridge behavior is compacted into TS bridge/serializer modules and exercised by FOL, TDFOL, and temporal native vectors.
Multiview bridge behavior is compact TS graph/view assembly covered through policy and bridge conformance vectors.
Bounded cache behavior is implemented through TS runtime cache/data structures rather than a same-name Python utility module.
Python error-class module is docstring-heavy; TS error utilities are intentionally smaller and sufficient for conformance paths.
Deontic formula building is compacted into TS formula builder/prover syntax code and exercised by deontic/legal native vectors.
Deontic IR types are represented by TS formula, legal norm, and prover syntax data structures rather than a same-name module.
Legal-text conversion behavior is split across TS deontic parser and bridge services with parser and bridge parity gates.
Deontic parser utilities are compact TS implementations with cross-language utility and element extraction parity gates.
End-to-end validation harness is test scaffolding, not runtime theorem-prover behavior; conformance replaces it with executable parity gates.
Lazy installer manages external prover binaries and is not counted as self-contained TS theorem-prover semantics.
Text-to-FOL conversion is represented by TS policy/FOL/TDFOL conversion and native FOL vectors.
Prover installer behavior is external binary management and not accepted as simulated prover parity.
Symbolic contract domain objects are folded into TS policy/legal norm structures and bridge outputs.
UCAN policy compilation is compacted into the TS NL UCAN compiler and exercised through policy/native conformance vectors.
Deontic conflict mixin behavior is integrated into TS TDFOL/deontic conflict decision logic.
Thin compatibility re-export is represented by the consolidated TS symbolic contract structures.
Codec is still compact relative to Python, but deterministic IR, guidance, and citation helpers now have direct TS implementations with cross-language parity gates.
Compiler is compact relative to Python, with deterministic family-token and serialization behavior directly ported and cross-language gated.
Decompiler is compact relative to Python, with deterministic token/slot and citation helper behavior directly ported and cross-language gated.
Phase benchmark harness is measurement scaffolding, not prover semantics; native conformance vectors cover behavioral decisions.
Input validation is folded into TS parser/hub boundaries and covered by adversarial native vectors.
Rate limiting is service policy, not theorem-prover semantics; prover behavior remains covered by native vectors.
Registry metadata is not prover semantics; compact TS registry behavior is sufficient for conformance surfaces.
Groth16 backup backend is external cryptographic prover infrastructure and cannot be accepted as simulated TS parity.
Groth16 FFI is host-native cryptographic prover integration; simulated parity is excluded by the ZKP host-native gate.
ProveKit FFI is host-native cryptographic prover integration; simulated parity is excluded by the ZKP host-native gate.
Ethereum contract artifact handling belongs to host/native deployment integration, not self-contained theorem-prover semantics.
EVM harness depends on host execution tooling and is not counted as simulated TS prover parity.
EVM public-input handling is host-native ZKP integration support and excluded from simulated prover parity.
ZKP proving requires real Groth16/ProveKit host-native backends; conformance excludes simulated ZKP parity from matches.
